Add mutex FFI, C shim, proptest, fuzz, and Kani harnesses

Phase 1 mutex FFI: gale_mutex_lock_validate and gale_mutex_unlock_validate
replace ownership checks and lock_count arithmetic in kernel/mutex.c with
formally verified Rust. Wait queue, scheduling, and priority inheritance
remain native Zephyr C.

Test coverage:
- 88 tests pass (unit + integration + proptest for both sem and mutex)
- 59 Verus verified, 0 errors
- 16/16 mutex integration tests pass under Miri (no UB)
- 13 Kani bounded model checking harnesses for mutex FFI
- 2 fuzz targets (mutex_fuzz, mutex_api_fuzz)
- 6 proptest properties (M1 invariant, roundtrip, reentrancy, contention)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Click to focus Electron app input before paste in executeMacro

Electron apps (Codex, Cursor, etc.) don't automatically focus their
text input on activation. The paste keystroke goes nowhere. Fix: after
activating an Electron target, click the center-bottom of the front
window (where chat inputs typically live) to ensure the text field has
focus, then wait 150ms before pasting.

Native apps (Claude) skip this step — they focus the input on activate.

Fix multi-display window restore; add macro dispatch logging

Window restore: use AXRaise on the specific window via System Events
instead of `tell application id "X" to activate`, which lets macOS
pick a window on the wrong display. AXRaise forces the correct window
to the front regardless of which display it's on.

Macro dispatch: log every macro request (target app, text length,
text preview) so empty-text drops are visible in bridge output.

Fix isometric terrarium: preserve-3d chain for proper counter-rotation

Plants were squished because 3D context was flattened. Now
preserve-3d is set on all intermediate elements so the
rotateZ(-45deg) rotateX(-55deg) counter-rotation properly
undoes the ground tilt, making plants stand upright.

commit.message:
Add Yappy QR payment integration with server-to-ESP32 MQTT dispensing

Integrates Yappy (Panama mobile payment) as an alternative payment method:
- Supabase: Edge Function for QR generation, status polling, and payment confirmation
- Supabase: DB migration adding 'yappy' channel, yappy_sessions table, external_transaction_id
- Android: YappyRepository, QR code rendering (ZXing), Yappy UI flow in VendingScreen
- VPS: /api/credit/mqtt-push endpoint for server-side credit push without duplicate sale recording

When Yappy payment confirms (PAGADO), the Edge Function records the sale and pushes
credit to the ESP32 via MQTT, starting a new MDB session so the customer can re-select
their product for auto-dispensing (bypasses BLE/MDB session timeout).

commit.message:
Add SS304 Colour Physics tutorial (Chapter 7)

Three interactive canvases:
1. Thin-film interference colour spectrum strip (0–175 nm, labelled zones)
2. Peak temperature → cooling curve → oxide thickness → colour swatch
3. Power × speed grid map with crosshair + live colour swatch + readout

Physics model: Arrhenius k_p = 3.9e9 × exp(−12400/T) nm²/s, parabolic
growth integrated over exponential cooling (τ = 4s, calibrated to match
spec: 18% power, 200mm/s → ~91 nm → Deep Blue).

Also adds Chapter 7 card to learning-lasers/index.html.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

rendered via innerHTML in journal entries and detail modals (bakeName,
notes, styleName, ovenType) to prevent XSS injection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(dashboard-e2e): operator actions E2E suite (gate advance, HTTP endpoint, disabled states)

Closes bead: agent-flywheel-console-3qw.4.3 + closes epic 3qw.4

── test/e2e/dashboard/02-operator-actions.e2e.ts (new, 14 tests) ────────

Gate advance — local action, no SSH required (5 tests):
  Demonstrates the full end-to-end round-trip through the browser dashboard:
  UI interaction → HTTP POST /action → server-side advanceGate() (now wrapped
  in a db.transaction()) → snapshot broadcast → browser DOM update.

  - Verifies gate-submit is enabled when a run exists (actionStates logic)
  - Verifies initial phase is "plan" in the metric card
  - Gate advance to "beads": select option + form submit → waits for
    "gate.advance succeeded" in #action-log → asserts #metric-phase = "beads"
  - Gate advance to "swarm": same cycle, confirms phase chain works
  - Action log: both gate advances produce INFO entries in #action-log

HTTP /action endpoint contract (3 tests):
  Uses page.evaluate() to call fetch() from within the browser's origin so
  CORS is irrelevant and the URL is always correct:
  - POST /action gate.advance → { ok: true, action: "gate.advance",
    payload: { nextPhase: "beads" } }
  - POST /action with malformed JSON → HTTP 400 + { ok: false }
  - POST /action with unknown action type → { ok: false } (no panic)

Disabled action states (3 tests):
  Empty workspace (no run, no SSH, no agents):
  - #pause-button isDisabled() = true (swarm.pause requires active agents)
  - #gate-submit isDisabled() = true (gate.advance requires a run)
  - #gate-note has non-empty guidance text (operators see why it's unavailable)

Swarm control visibility (3 tests):
  With a "swarm" phase run seeded:
  - #pause-button exists in the DOM
  - #resume-button exists in the DOM
  - #swarm-controls-note has non-empty guidance text

── Key design patterns ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

waitForActionLog(h, text): waits for a matching entry in #action-log articles,
handling the async nature of runAction() (disabled button → fetch → logAction).

All beforeAll hooks use HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS = 30s to accommodate cold Playwright
startup; individual test timeouts use the Vitest-level 30s from CLI.

Epic 3qw.4 (Browser E2E) fully closed: 00-harness (setup infrastructure),
01-readonly-states (load/empty/populated/SSH states), 02-operator-actions
(gate advance, HTTP contract, disabled state verification).

Full suite: 786 passing, 39 skipped, 0 failing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix refresh_index page matching to handle all CustomGPT API field formats

The old code only matched pages by p.filename which returned deleted: 0
because the API may return the identifier in p.page_url instead, or as
a basename rather than a full relative path.

Now tries filename and page_url against both relative path and basename,
plus suffix matching for URL-prefixed values.

Soluciona la pantalla negra vacía tras el login.

commit.message:
init: Nyx Language v0.20.5 — compilador autónomo sin dependencias externas

Primer release del lenguaje Nyx (anteriormente Helix).
El compilador está escrito en Nyx y se compila a sí mismo (bootstrap).

Incluye:
- compiler/*.nx — fuentes del compilador en Nyx
- compiler/*.ll — IR semilla para reconstruir sin compilador previo
- runtime/*.c   — runtime en C (GC, strings, arrays, maps, file-io)
- tests/        — 21 tests de regresión
- Makefile      — make bootstrap | make run | make test
- docs/         — guía de inicio y contribución

Fixed point de segundo orden alcanzado. Compilador completamente autónomo.
Extensión de archivo: .nx | Runtime prefix: nyx_

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(dashboard-e2e): browser E2E harness + read-only state test suite

Closes beads: agent-flywheel-console-3qw.4.1 and 3qw.4.2

Adds a full Playwright-based browser E2E layer that tests the dashboard UI
against a real, in-process FlywheelServer using an isolated in-memory DB.
No VPS, no SSH, no external dependencies beyond @playwright/test.

── @playwright/test added as devDependency ──────────────────────────────

Playwright v1 provides chromium.launch() as a library API usable within
existing Vitest test suites. Tests run in the normal Vitest process alongside
all other suites. Chromium headless shell pre-installed via playwright install.

── test/e2e/dashboard/00-harness.ts (new) ───────────────────────────────

startDashboardHarness(opts?):
  - Creates a real FlywheelServer on port 0 with a fresh in-memory StateManager
  - Accepts optional seedState() callback to pre-populate the DB before start
  - Runs addInitScript() to inject the correct server URL into localStorage
    BEFORE the page loads — critical because the dashboard defaults its
    WebSocket connection to port 4200 (hardcoded in index.html); without this
    injection the WebSocket would connect to the wrong port and the ws-status
    element would never reach "Live"
  - Launches a headless Chromium BrowserContext (1280×900 viewport)
  - Captures all browser console messages (type + text + timestamp)
  - Captures all failed network requests (4xx/5xx + connection errors)
  - Returns { page, baseUrl, wsUrl, consoleLogs, failedRequests, screenshot(),
    saveLogs(), teardown() }
  - teardown() is idempotent — safe to call multiple times in afterAll

screenshot(name):
  - Saves a full-page PNG to test-artifacts/<date>/dashboard/<suite>/
  - Returns the absolute path for assertion (verifies save succeeded)
  - Increments a counter prefix so screenshots sort chronologically

saveLogs(testTitle):
  - Writes a structured log file alongside screenshots containing all
    captured console messages and failed requests

withFailureScreenshot(harness, name, fn):
  - Decorator that wraps a test body: calls harness.screenshot("FAIL-<name>")
    before rethrowing any error so failures are always captured visually

── test/e2e/dashboard/01-readonly-states.e2e.ts (new, 18 tests) ─────────

Uses beforeAll/afterAll (one server+browser per describe block) rather than
beforeEach/afterEach. Playwright browser startup takes ~2s; paying that cost
once per group rather than per test brings the full suite runtime to ~9s.

Each beforeAll uses a 30s hook timeout; individual test assertions use
waitForFunction() with a 12s timeout (enough for loopback WebSocket connect).

Empty workspace (8 tests):
  - Page title is "Flywheel Dashboard"
  - #ws-status contains "Live" (WebSocket connected to test server)
  - #run-badge contains "No run" (snapshot.run is null)
  - #metric-run shows "—", #metric-phase shows "—", #metric-agents shows "0"
  - #metric-beads shows "—" when no run exists
  - #server-meta matches /Server 127.0.0.1:\d+/

Populated state (5 tests):
  - DB seeded with a flywheel_run("my-project", "swarm") + bead snapshot
  - #metric-run shows 8 hex chars (shortenId() clips UUID to 8 chars; no ellipsis)
  - #metric-project contains "my-project"
  - #metric-phase shows "swarm"
  - #run-badge does NOT contain "No run"
  - Full-page screenshot saved

SSH disconnected (2 tests):
  - No SSH yaml → server's sshManager.isConnected() is always false
  - #metric-ssh shows "Disconnected"
  - #metric-agents shows "0" (NTM poll never fires without SSH)

Browser console hygiene (2 tests):
  - Zero pageerror events in console after initial load
  - Zero 4xx/5xx responses (favicon excluded from assertion)

withFailureScreenshot utility (1 test):
  - Calls at test-run time (not declaration) so harness is guaranteed set
  - Happy path: no FAIL- screenshot written when test passes

── vitest.config.ts — add dashboard e2e glob ────────────────────────────

  "test/e2e/dashboard/**/*.e2e.ts" added to the include list so npm test
  picks up the suite automatically alongside other local e2e suites.

Full suite: 772 passing, 39 skipped. No regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

backend/requirements.txt to resolve "gunicorn: command not found" error.

* fix: remove duplicate DATABASES config breaking Railway

Railway provides DATABASE_URL env var, but duplicate DATABASES block
(lines 194-210) required individual DATABASE_NAME/USER/PASSWORD vars.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: switch to Railpack builder with automatic migrations (#118)

- Change builder from NIXPACKS to RAILPACK in railway.json
- Add collectstatic to Procfile release phase (after migrate)
- Archive nixpacks.toml as .backup (no longer used)

Railpack will now execute migrations automatically before each deploy via Procfile release phase.

* chore: add releaseCommand to Railway config for migrations

Railway needs releaseCommand to run python manage.py migrate before
container starts. With Railpack + backend root, the repo-root railway.json
wasn't being picked up, so DB migrations never ran.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use preDeployCommand instead of releaseCommand in Railway config

releaseCommand is not a valid field in Railway's railway.json.
The correct field name for Railpack is preDeployCommand, which runs
before the container starts. This ensures migrations run before
Django tries to serve requests.

commit.message:
Add verified mutex (kernel/mutex.c port); 59 verified, 0 errors

Verus-verified reentrant mutex with ownership tracking, matching
Zephyr's k_mutex semantics: init, try_lock, lock_blocking, unlock.

Verified properties (ASIL-D):
  M1: lock_count > 0 ⟺ owner.is_some()
  M2: waiters ⟹ locked
  M3-M5: lock acquire (unlocked/reentrant/contended)
  M6: unlock by non-owner returns EPERM/EINVAL
  M7-M9: unlock (reentrant release/transfer/full unlock)
  M10: no overflow  M11: wait queue ordering preserved

Adds: src/mutex.rs (Verus), plain/src/mutex.rs (Rocq mirror),
tests/mutex_integration.rs (16 integration tests), EPERM error code.

fix: SOL missing from portfolio — fetch price from Jupiter, not DexScreener

DexScreener's /tokens/{mint} batch endpoint returns no pairs for the native
SOL mint because SOL is almost always the quote token in Solana pairs.
This caused market['SOL'] to be undefined → price=0 → value=0 → filtered
out by the h.value >= 0.01 gate in portfolio.js.

- Add fetchSolPriceJupiter() with 60s cache, fetches in parallel with the
  DexScreener ecosystem batch
- fetchSolanaMarketMap: guarantee map['SOL'] is always populated; if
  DexScreener happened to return SOL pair data, keep its change1h/24h
  but override price with Jupiter (more accurate); otherwise create a
  SOL-only entry from Jupiter price
- Remove SOL from TOP_SOLANA_MINTS — dedicated Jupiter fetch is more reliable

repository.name: mercer-agent
repository.description: Autonomous DeFi portfolio agent for Solana. Powered by Claude.

commit.message:
fix(peer-review): three bugs found in deep first-principles review

1. state.ts — advanceGate() missing transaction (atomicity bug)

The gate-advance path performed three separate database operations:
  (a) SELECT flywheel_runs to capture previousPhase
  (b) UPDATE flywheel_runs SET phase, gate_passed_at, checkpoint_sha
  (c) INSERT into phase_events (via logEvent)

With no wrapping transaction, a process crash between (b) and (c) would
leave flywheel_runs updated to the new phase but no gate_advanced event
in the audit log. Callers of flywheel replay and the dashboard would see
the phase jump without a matching event, making root-cause debugging
unreliable and the event log an inaccurate source of truth.

Fixed by wrapping all three operations in this.db.transaction()(), which
better-sqlite3 executes atomically under BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT.

Note: better-sqlite3 is synchronous and single-threaded, so the failure
window is a process crash (SIGKILL, OOM) rather than a concurrent write.
The transaction guarantees both succeed or both roll back.

2. runs.ts — durationStr() returns negative strings on clock skew

new Date(completedAt) - new Date(startedAt) can be negative when:
  - System clock jumps backward between run start and completion
  - Database row is corrupted (e.g. completedAt copied from a different run)
  - Timestamps from two machines with unsynchronized clocks

Without a guard, Math.floor(-45000 / 1000) = -45 and the output is "-45s",
which renders in the `flywheel runs` table as a confusing negative duration.

Fixed with Math.max(0, ...) on the raw millisecond difference. A 0ms
duration ("0s") is a safe sentinel for all out-of-order timestamp cases.

3. state.ts — missing indexes on api_calls and bead_snapshots run_id

The schema had idx_phase_events_run_ts but no indexes on the other two
tables that are queried per-run:

  getTotalCost()       → SELECT SUM(cost_usd) FROM api_calls WHERE run_id = ?
  getApiCalls()        → SELECT * FROM api_calls WHERE run_id = ?
  beadVelocity()       → SELECT * FROM bead_snapshots WHERE run_id = ? LIMIT N
  getBeadSnapshots()   → SELECT * FROM bead_snapshots WHERE run_id = ?

All four perform a full table scan in the absence of an index. For small
datasets (< 10,000 rows) SQLite's page cache makes this imperceptible.
For a long-running autopilot session that logs API calls every few minutes
or a project with hundreds of bead snapshots, getTotalCost() (called on
every status poll) and beadVelocity() (called on every dashboard refresh)
degrade linearly with table size.

Added:
  idx_api_calls_run_id ON api_calls(run_id)
  idx_bead_snapshots_run_id ON bead_snapshots(run_id, captured_at)

The composite (run_id, captured_at) index on bead_snapshots also
accelerates the ORDER BY captured_at DESC LIMIT N in beadVelocity()
without a separate sort pass.

Both use IF NOT EXISTS so they apply to existing databases on next open.

Also: close epic agent-flywheel-console-3qw.6 — all three transport
integration test children (6.1 SSHManager, 6.2 RemoteRunner, 6.3
NtmBridge) completed in this session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Increase scroll padding so cards can reach top on desktop multi-column layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add AI-generated match analysis via Claude API

- New app/services/analysis.py: generates individual match analysis text
  using claude-haiku for each match in parallel (asyncio.gather)
- Uses quantitative features (Elo, form, H2H, position, rest, DC ratings)
  to explain WHY the model predicts what it does
- In-memory cache per (matchday, matchId) to avoid regenerating on refresh
- Graceful fallback: returns empty string if API key not set
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY added to config (set via Railway env var)
- analysisText: str | None added to MatchPrediction schema
- iOS: analysisText field added to MatchPrediction model
- iOS: "KI-Analyse" card shown in MatchDetailView when text is available

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

checkpoint feedback #5: M4 evaluering noteret

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: re-dispatch planning zouzhe after timeout retry

check_timeouts clears dispatched_at on retry, but poll_and_dispatch
only picks up created→planning and revising→planning transitions.
A zouzhe already in 'planning' state with dispatched_at=NULL (from
retry) was never re-dispatched. Add step 2a to handle this case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: log missing DexScreener pairs, auto-include held positions, USDC hardcoded

- fetchSolanaEcosystem: log [No pair found for: SYMBOL (mint...)] for any
  hardcoded token that returns no DexScreener pairs, making gaps visible
- loadHeldSupplementalMints: reads entry-prices.json + token-registry-cache.json
  on each fetch to auto-include held positions not in TOP_SOLANA_MINTS —
  EAT (375ai mint) and ZAUTH (zauthx402 mint) will now be batch-fetched
- fetchSolanaMarketMap: inject USDC_ENTRY (price=1) directly — USDC never
  falls through to Jupiter fallback pricing
